documented_expected_behaviour: Convert subjective culture language into observable workplace behaviours.
Avoid assessing similarity, likeability, or background resemblance.
Link behaviours to role situations and ZURU DNA.
initial_manager_statement: Skills can be taught. I need someone who gets our vibe.

turns.manager_message: Skills can be taught. I need someone who gets our vibe.

next_question.question: What observable, job-relevant behaviours would show that someone “gets our vibe”?

turns.manager_message: You know, just someone who'd fit right in with the team at the pub on a Friday, laughs at the same jokes, doesn't take themselves too seriously. If I click with them in the interview, that's usually a good sign.

next_question.question: What specific observable, job-related collaboration or communication behaviours do you need from this Brand Coordinator?

turns.manager_message: Okay, concretely: when a campaign deadline moves up and priorities clash, they need to say so directly to the team instead of quietly resenting it, and they should offer to help a teammate who's behind even when it's not their job.

next_question.question: What specific channels or escalation approach should they use to raise campaign deadline and priority conflicts with the team?

turns.manager_message: They should raise it in our weekly stand-up or directly to me, not just vent to teammates. And yes, this connects to being a good human on the team and pulling together, not just being liked.

next_question.question: What specific observable teamwork behaviours should demonstrate “pulling together” in this role?

rejected_turns.note: The same must-have/ambiguity overlap guard also fired here, on a compound behavioural answer (two distinct behavioural expectations in one sentence). A retry against the same input passed the next attempt, confirming the pass/fail split is due to provider-side sampling variance on an otherwise fixed prompt, not the input itself. See docs/DECISIONS_AND_LESSONS.md entry 2026-07-24.
rejected_turns.manager_message: Okay, concretely: when a campaign deadline moves up and priorities clash, they need to say so directly to the team instead of quietly resenting it, and they should offer to help a teammate who's behind even when it's not their job.

basic_info.title: Brand Coordinator
basic_info.initial_manager_statement: Skills can be taught. I need someone who gets our vibe.

requirements.name: Directly communicate deadline and priority conflicts
requirements.description: Communicate directly to the team when a campaign deadline moves up and priorities clash, rather than withholding concerns.
requirements.business_rationale: The manager states that the person needs to raise these conflicts directly with the team.
requirements.source_statement: when a campaign deadline moves up and priorities clash, they need to say so directly to the team instead of quietly resenting it

requirements.name: Proactively support behind teammates
requirements.description: Offer help to a teammate who is behind, including when the work is outside the person's formal job responsibilities.
requirements.business_rationale: The manager says the person should offer help in this situation, using 'should' rather than an explicit mandatory statement.
requirements.source_statement: they should offer to help a teammate who's behind even when it's not their job

requirements.name: Raise issues through appropriate channels
requirements.description: Raise relevant issues in the weekly stand-up or directly to the manager rather than only venting to teammates.
requirements.business_rationale: The manager specifies the expected ways for issues to be raised and contrasts them with only venting to teammates.
requirements.source_statement: They should raise it in our weekly stand-up or directly to me, not just vent to teammates.

open_ambiguities.description: “Gets our vibe” is subjective culture-fit language and does not identify observable, job-relevant behaviours or working expectations. The manager needs to clarify the specific behaviours, collaboration style, or brand-related understanding required.
open_ambiguities.source_statement: Skills can be taught. I need someone who gets our vibe.
open_ambiguities.why_confirmation_is_needed: It cannot be translated into a fair, assessable role requirement without concrete examples.

open_ambiguities.description: The message uses subjective social and interview-rapport preferences rather than observable, job-relevant behaviours. The manager needs to clarify which work-related collaboration, communication, or brand-related behaviours are required.
open_ambiguities.source_statement: You know, just someone who'd fit right in with the team at the pub on a Friday, laughs at the same jokes, doesn't take themselves too seriously. If I click with them in the interview, that's usually a good sign.
open_ambiguities.why_confirmation_is_needed: Social similarity, humour, and interviewer rapport are not defined job requirements and need to be translated into observable work expectations.

open_ambiguities.description: The reference to “being a good human on the team and pulling together” is subjective and does not specify observable, job-relevant collaborative behaviours. The manager needs to clarify the specific teamwork behaviours expected.
open_ambiguities.source_statement: And yes, this connects to being a good human on the team and pulling together, not just being liked.
open_ambiguities.why_confirmation_is_needed: This phrasing could refer to different expectations, such as helping colleagues, communicating constructively, or contributing during shared work, and cannot be assessed consistently without defined behaviours.

blockers: At least one success outcome is required.
At least one responsibility is required.
At least one assessment method is required.
A human decision owner is required.
